channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Bolivia holds a divisive popular vote for its top judges, offering lessons for the region
item.description: LA PAZ, Bolivia (AP) — Campaigning for Sunday's judicial election may be strictly forbidden, but look closer on the streets of Bolivia’s capital, La Paz, and you’ll find that some candidates have sneakily plastered their faces on packs of corn puffs and others have slipped subtle slogans into offici
item.dc:creator: PAOLA FLORES and ISABEL DEBRE, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Activists release images of foxes at Finnish fur farms to push EU to ban the trade
item.description: Finnish advocacy group Oikeutta Elaimille, or Justice for Animals, and Humane Society International have released images taken from an “undercover investigation” at three fur farms in western Finland in late October to highlight the behind-the-scenes realities of the trade.
item.dc:creator: Jamey Keaten, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump appoints longtime foreign policy adviser Richard Grenell to serve as special missions envoy
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump has picked longtime foreign policy adviser Richard Grenell to serve as an envoy for special missions.
item.dc:creator: Meg Kinnard And Aamer Madhani, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: South Korea’s parliament votes to impeach President Yoon Suk Yeol over his martial law order
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea’s parliament on Saturday impeached President Yoon Suk Yeol over his stunning and short-lived martial law decree, a move that ended days of political paralysis but set up an intense debate over Yoon's fate, as jubilant crowds roared to celebrate another defiant m
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ex-soccer player Kavelashvili becomes Georgia's president in a blow to country's EU aspirations
item.description: TBILISI, Georgia (AP) — Former soccer player Mikheil Kavelashvili became president of Georgia on Saturday, as the ruling party tightened its grip in what the opposition calls a blow to the country’s EU aspirations and a victory for former imperial ruler Russia.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIKO MEGRELIDZE, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: US officials have been in direct contact with the Syrian rebel group that ousted Assad, Blinken says
item.description: AQABA, Jordan (AP) — American officials have been in direct contact with the terrorist-designated rebel group that led the overthrow of Syrian President Bashar Assad, U.S. Secretary of State Antony Blinken said Saturday. Blinken, speaking at a news conference in Jordan, was the first U.S.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Isak Andic, founder of Spanish fashion brand Mango, dies in accident, aged 71
item.description: Isak Andic, the founder of Spanish fashion brand Mango, has died after suffering an accident, the company said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Women's right activists rally in France ahead of Gisèle Pelicot's rape trial verdict
item.description: AVIGNON, France (AP) — Hundreds of people gathered Saturday in the southern French town of Avignon ahead of the verdict in a trial in which dozens of men are accused of raping Gisèle Pelicot while she was drugged and rendered unconscious by her husband.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Filipino woman on Indonesia death row recalls a stunning last minute reprieve and 'miracle' transfer
item.description: Filipino death row inmate Mary Jane Fiesta Veloso knelt to pray when officers came to take her to an execution site in May 2015, just feet from her isolation cell on an Indonesian prison island, where a 13-member firing squad was waiting.
item.dc:creator: Slamet Riyadi, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Pelosi 'on the mend' after hip replacement surgery in Germany
item.description: Former House Speaker Nancy Pelosi (D-Calif.), who was hospitalized during a trip to Luxembourg, underwent hip surgery early Saturday, according to a spokesperson.
item.dc:creator: Steff Danielle Thomas, The Hill

item.title: Ukrainian drones strike Russia as Kyiv reels from consecutive massive air attacks
item.description: Ukrainian drone strikes on southern Russia have killed a 9-year-old boy and set fire to a major oil terminal.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Middle East latest: Top diplomats from US, Arab League and Turkey discuss Syria's transition
item.description: Top diplomats from the United States, the Arab League and Turkey have met in Jordan to discuss Syria’s transition after the fall of Bashar Assad's government last weekend.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: A look at the whirlwind events that led to the impeachment of South Korea's president
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea’s parliament on Saturday voted to impeach embattled President Yoon Suk Yeol over his short-lived martial law decree this month, a shocking stunt that paralyzed the country’s politics, halted foreign policy and spooked financial markets.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Korea lawmakers vote to impeach president over martial law declaration
item.description: South Korean lawmakers on Saturday voted to impeach President Yoon Suk Yeol over his short-lived martial law declaration earlier this month.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: UN talks fail to reach agreement on dealing with rising risk of global drought
item.description: BENGALURU, India (AP) — Despite two weeks of U.N.-sponsored talks in Saudi Arabia’s Riyadh, the participating 197 nations failed to agree early Saturday on a plan to deal with global droughts, made longer and more severe by a warming climate.
item.dc:creator: SIBI ARASU, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: New Zealand scientists suspect specimen of world’s rarest whale died from head injuries
item.description: After completing the dissection of the first complete specimen of the world's rarest whale, scientists suspect the 16-foot spade-toothed whale died from head injuries.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Turkey seeks to purge pro-US Kurdish force that helped defeat Islamic State in Syria
item.description: A Turkish strongman exploits chaos in Syria to oust pro-American Syrian Kurds from border area while solidifying expansion in war-torn nation
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Syrians cheer end of 50 years of Assad rule at first Friday prayers since government fell
item.description: DAMASCUS (AP) — Exuberant Syrians observed the first Friday prayers since the ouster of President Bashar Assad, gathering in the capital's historic main mosque, its largest square and around the country to celebrate the end of half a century of authoritarian rule.
item.dc:creator: ALBERT AJI and MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Former Syrian prison official charged in US with torture
item.description: A former Syrian official under the rule of former President Bashar Assad was federally indicted in the U.S. for torturing prisoners held at Damascus Central Prison from 2005-08, according to a Thursday release from the Justice Department. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Israel eyes Iran nuke sites amid reports Trump mulls moves to block Tehran atomic program
item.description: Israel is once again said to be eyeing strikes on Iran's nuclear program as the incoming Trump administration is reportedly mulling plans on how to come down hard on Iran without prompting a war.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: EU nations agree a crackdown on migrant smugglers. Some fear it may target people trying to help
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — European Union countries agreed on Friday a draft law aimed at preventing and countering migrant smuggling, which critics say could be used to target people or charity groups that try to help migrants in trouble.
item.dc:creator: LORNE COOK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Suspected Chinese spy with business ties to Prince Andrew barred from UK
item.description: LONDON (AP) — A suspected Chinese spy with business ties to Prince Andrew has been barred from the U.K. because of concerns he poses a threat to national security.
item.dc:creator: DANICA KIRKA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: China's military is staying silent on its recent activity around Taiwan
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — China's military kept silent Friday on its large deployment of naval and coast guard ships off Taiwan this week, with its top spokesperson quoting an ancient strategist instead.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ontario to restrict electricity exports to US and bar American-made alcohol if Trump tariffs applied
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Canada’s most populous province may bar American-made alcohol in addition to restricting electricity exports to Michigan, New York and Minnesota if U.S. President-elect Donald Trump imposes sweeping tariffs on all Canadian products, a senior official said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Environmental groups condemn new laws threatening soybean restrictions in Brazil's Amazon
item.description: BRASILIA, Brazil (AP) — Dozens of environmental nonprofits issued a manifesto Thursday condemning new laws in Brazilian states that threaten to dismantle the Amazon soy moratorium — a landmark voluntary agreement banning trade in soybeans from recently deforested areas.
item.dc:creator: FABIANO MAISONNAVE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: In Lesotho, UN chief Guterres urges rich nations to meet new commitments on climate finance
item.description: MASERU, Lesotho (AP) — United Nations Secretary-General António Guterres on Thursday urged rich nations to honor their commitments to help the world's poor countries fight climate change in a speech to Lesotho's Parliament and repeated his hope that Africa would soon have permanent seats on the U.N.
item.dc:creator: KEKETSO PHAKELA and GERALD IMRAY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trudeau: Harris loss a setback for women in politics
item.description: Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau on Tuesday addressed Vice President Harris’s loss in the 2024 presidential election, calling it a setback for gender representation in politics, during a gala promoting women in politics.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: American freed after 7 months detention in Syria
item.description: An American citizen detained in Syria was freed Thursday after rebels overtook the city of Damascus, toppling the rule of President Bashar Assad and releasing thousands from imprisonment according to The Associated Press. Pete Travis Timmerman, 29, from Urbana, Mo.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: American who says he crossed into Syria by foot is free after 7 months
item.description: An American who turned up in Syria on Thursday says he was detained after crossing into the country by foot on a Christian pilgrimage seven months ago.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Coffee prices hit record high after challenging growing season
item.description: Arabica coffee sold for $3.44 a pound on the global market earlier this week, beating the prior record high of $3.35 set in 1977.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: North Korea condemns South Korea as 'fascist dictatorship' after martial law scare
item.description: North Korean dictator Kim Jong Un denounced South Korea as a 'fascist dictatorship' following President Yoon Suk Yeol's failed attempt to impose martial law.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Blinken testifies at hearing on Afghanistan withdrawal
item.description: Lawmakers have been demanding answers into how such a significant operation could have gone so wrong.
item.dc:creator: Elina Shirazi, NewsNation

item.title: Israel's Netanyahu vows to challenge 'absurd' corruption charges as he takes the stand in his trial
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu promised defiantly on Tuesday to knock down corruption allegations against him as he began testifying in his long-running trial, becoming the first sitting Israeli leader to take the stand as a criminal defendant.
item.dc:creator: TIA GOLDENBERG, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: China’s sci-fi spherical Death Star-like robot cop uses AI, facial recognition to track criminals
item.description: Chinese robotics company, Logon Technology, unveiled the RT-G autonomous spherical robot which will use AI and facial recognition to aid law enforcement.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: South Korea's Justice Ministry imposes a travel ban on President Yoon over martial law
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea’s Justice Ministry on Monday imposed an overseas travel ban on President Yoon Suk Yeol as authorities investigate allegations of rebellion and other charges in connection with his short-lived declaration of martial law last week.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Syrian community in Chicago celebrates end of Assad regime
item.description: It was a sea of green flags on Michigan Avenue Sunday as hundreds of Chicagoans celebrated the end of the regime in Syria.
item.dc:creator: Emmy Victor, NewsNation

item.title: Ousted Syrian leader Assad flees to Moscow after fall of Damascus, Russian state media say
item.description: DAMASCUS, Syria (AP) — Ousted Syrian leader Bashar Assad fled to Moscow and received asylum from his longtime ally, Russian media said Sunday, hours after a stunning rebel advance seized control of Damascus and ended his family’s 50 years of iron rule.
item.dc:creator: ABDULRAHMAN SHAHEEN, SARAH EL DEEB and ABBY SEWELL, Associated Press, NewsNation
